textEn:
Find out the correct sentence :

(a) You will be suspended if you pay your fees.

(b) You will be suspended unless you don't pay your fees.

(c) You will be suspended if you had paid your fees.

(d) You will be suspended unless you pay your fees.
textHi:
Find out the correct sentence :

(a) You will be suspended if you pay your fees.

(b) You will be suspended unless you don't pay your fees.

(c) You will be suspended if you had paid your fees.

(d) You will be suspended unless you pay your fees.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "(d) You will be suspended unless you pay your fees. "

Key Points

  • The sentence needs to express a conditional situation where not doing something (paying fees) leads to suspension.
  • This is typically expressed with "unless" to mean "if not."
  • Option (d) "You will be suspended unless you pay your fees." correctly uses "unless" to indicate that suspension can be avoided if the fees are paid.
    • It correctly communicates the condition to avoid suspension.
  • Option (a) suggests that suspension occurs because fees are paid, which contradicts the logical sequence of such situations.
  • Option (b) is incorrect because "unless you don't" is a double negative that can cause confusion.
    • The correct phrase is "unless you" followed by the positive form of the verb.
  • Option (c) uses past tense "had paid", which is inappropriate since the sentence is predicting a future consequence based on a present or future condition (paying fees).
  • Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 3".
explanationHi:
The correct answer is "(d) You will be suspended unless you pay your fees. "

Key Points

  • The sentence needs to express a conditional situation where not doing something (paying fees) leads to suspension.
  • This is typically expressed with "unless" to mean "if not."
  • Option (d) "You will be suspended unless you pay your fees." correctly uses "unless" to indicate that suspension can be avoided if the fees are paid.
    • It correctly communicates the condition to avoid suspension.
  • Option (a) suggests that suspension occurs because fees are paid, which contradicts the logical sequence of such situations.
  • Option (b) is incorrect because "unless you don't" is a double negative that can cause confusion.
    • The correct phrase is "unless you" followed by the positive form of the verb.
  • Option (c) uses past tense "had paid", which is inappropriate since the sentence is predicting a future consequence based on a present or future condition (paying fees).
  • Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 3".

textEn:
Directions: In this section, each sentence is incomplete and is followed by four options. Select one of the four options to complete the sentence.

He excels in his studies _________.
textHi:
Directions: In this section, each sentence is incomplete and is followed by four options. Select one of the four options to complete the sentence.

He excels in his studies _________.
optionsEn: so that he can secure a scholarship.
although he works part-time.
but he struggles with time management.
as if he were failing his classes.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "so that he can secure a scholarship."


Key Points

  • This sentence suggests a purpose or intention behind excelling in studies. The phrase "so that" introduces the purpose of an action clearly and is the most fitting connector for indicating intent or aim.
  • While options 2 and 3 introduce contrast and option 4 introduces a hypothetical situation, none of them directly link to the notion of excelling for a specific beneficial outcome, such as securing a scholarship.
  • The phrase "so that" implies a direct, positive outcome (securing a scholarship) as a result of the action (excelling in studies), adhering to a cause-and-effect structure with a specific goal in mind.
  • This completion underscores strategic effort, where excelling in studies is not an end in itself but a means to achieve another significant goal.
Hence, the correct answer is 'Option 1'.

The correct answer is "so that he can secure a scholarship."

textEn:
Consider the following sentences:

A) I love Ethan Hunt more than you.

B) I was scared when I noticed a stranger who was following me.

Which of the following is the correct observation about the above two sentences?
textHi:
Consider the following sentences:

A) I love Ethan Hunt more than you.

B) I was scared when I noticed a stranger who was following me.

Which of the following is the correct observation about the above two sentences?
optionsEn: (A) is ambiguous and (B) is grammatically correct.
(A) is ambiguous and (B) is grammatically incorrect.
(A) is unambiguous and (B) is grammatically incorrect.
(A) is unambiguous and (B) is grammatically correct.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is '(A) is unambiguous and (B) is grammatically correct'.

Key Points

  • Sentence A says: I love Ethan Hunt > I love you.
    • Thus, there is no other way in which it can be interpreted. So, it is unambiguous.
  • Sentence B says: I was scared because of being followed by a stranger.
    • There is no grammatical mistake or any ambiguity in the sentence.

Thus, the correct answer is option 4).

  • One might think that Sentence A should be interpreted as following:
    • I love Ethan Hunt > You love Ethan Hunt.
  • ​But the above meaning would be possible if Sentence A were: I love Ethan Hunt more than you do.

textEn:
Change the following sentence from active to passive:

They clean the house every week.
textHi:
Change the following sentence from active to passive:

They clean the house every week.
optionsEn: The house is cleaned by them every week.
The house was cleaned by them every week.
Every week the house was cleaned by them.
Cleaning the house was their weekly task.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is: 'The house is cleaned by them every week.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in the Present Simple Tense in Active Voice.
  • To change a Present Simple Tense from Active voice to Passive voice: Interchange the object and the subject with each other - object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence.
  • Structure:
    • Subject + V1+s/es + object (Active Voice)
    • Object + is/are + V3 + by Subject (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • They clean the house every week. (Active Voice)
    • The house is cleaned by them every week. (Passive Voice)
  • The subject of the given sentence is 'They'.
  • The object of the given sentence is 'the house'.
  • Apply the rule changes - 'clean' will be changed into 'is cleaned'.

Correct sentence: The house is cleaned by them every week.

Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 1.'
explanationHi:
The correct answer is: 'The house is cleaned by them every week.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in the Present Simple Tense in Active Voice.
  • To change a Present Simple Tense from Active voice to Passive voice: Interchange the object and the subject with each other - object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence.
  • Structure:
    • Subject + V1+s/es + object (Active Voice)
    • Object + is/are + V3 + by Subject (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • They clean the house every week. (Active Voice)
    • The house is cleaned by them every week. (Passive Voice)
  • The subject of the given sentence is 'They'.
  • The object of the given sentence is 'the house'.
  • Apply the rule changes - 'clean' will be changed into 'is cleaned'.

Correct sentence: The house is cleaned by them every week.

Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 1.'

Therefore, the correct answer is: Option 3 i.e. clique.

Additional Information

  • Pique (आक्रोश): A feeling of irritation or resentment resulting from a slight, especially to one's pride.
    • Example: She left the meeting in a fit of pique after her ideas were dismissed.
  • Antique (पुराना): A collectible object such as a piece of furniture or work of art that has a high value because of its considerable age.
    • Example: She collects antique jewelry from the Victorian era.

textEn:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice.

My brother was being laughed at by them.
textHi:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice.

My brother was being laughed at by them.
optionsEn: They have been laughing at my brother.
They were laughing at my brother.
They had been laughing at my brother.
explanationEn:
Here the correct answer is They were laughing at my brother.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in passive voice. We need to change it into active voice.
  • The subject 'my brother' becomes the object of the active voice and the object 'them' becomes the subject 'they' of the active voice.
  • 'was being laughed' changes to 'were laughing'.
  • Structure of Past Continuous Tense:
    • Passive Voice: Object + was/were + being + V3 + by + Subject.
    • Active Voice: Subject + was/were + V1+ing + Object.
  • Thus, the correct answer is Option 2.


Correct Active Voice:

  • For example:

    • I felt embarrassed when I realized they were laughing at me.

textEn:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.

The thief asked if he remembered the place where they had hidden their jewellery.
textHi:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.

The thief asked if he remembered the place where they had hidden their jewellery.
optionsEn: The thief asked, "Where do you remember the place where we have hidden our jewellery?"
The thief asked, "Can you remember the place where we were hiding our jewellery?"
The thief asked, "Do you remember the place where we have hidden our jewellery?"
The thief asked, "Did you remember the place where we hid our jewellery?"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "Option 3".

Key Points

  • The sentence "The thief asked if he remembered the place where they had hidden their jewellery" is a reported speech, and the goal here is to change it back into direct speech.
  • When transforming reported speech back into direct speech, we need to change the tense back to its original form.
  • The verb "remember" remains the same as it's a mental verb which doesn't usually change in reported speech.
  • In the original sentence, "had hidden" is in the past perfect tense, which usually transforms back into present perfect in direct speech, thus "have hidden" is the correct tense to use.

Therefore, the correct answer is: The thief asked, "Do you remember the place where we have hidden our jewellery?"
explanationHi:
The correct answer is "Option 3".

Therefore, the correct answer is: The thief asked, "Do you remember the place where we have hidden our jewellery?"

textEn:
In the following question, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Indirect/Direct speech.

"Please don't leave me alone here", the kid told her mother.
textHi:
In the following question, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Indirect/Direct speech.

"Please don't leave me alone here", the kid told her mother.
optionsEn: The kid begged her mother not to leave her alone there.
The kid begs her mother not to leave her alone there.
The kid begged her mother not to leave her alone here.
The kid begs her mother not to leave her alone here.
explanationEn:
The correct option is The kid begged her mother not to leave her alone there.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Direct speech, and we have to convert it into Indirect speech.
  • In indirect speech, the tense, pronouns, and time expressions are changed according to the rules of reported speech.
  • First, we change the reporting verb "told" into the reporting verb "begged" to convey the sense of request in the sentence.
  • Then we change the pronoun "me" to "her" because we are reporting someone else's words.
  • Next, we have to change the tense of the reported speech.
  • don't changes to 'not to'.
  • 'to' is followed by V1 form of the verb i.e. leave.
  • Finally, we change "here" to "there" because we are reporting what someone said in a different location.
  • Thus, the correct answer is Option 1.

Therefore, the correct conversion is: The kid begged her mother not to leave her alone there.

Additional Information

  • The original direct speech is in present tense and indicates a kid requesting her mother not to leave her alone.
  • Option 2 is not suitable because it keeps the tense of the statement the same without changing it to past tense and so is incorrect.
  • Option 3 is not suitable because it keeps the location 'here' as it is but in indirect tense it should change to 'there' thus making it incorrect.
  • Option 4 is not suitable because it changes the tense to present tense but in indirect speech it should be past tense, making it the incorrect option.
  • Hence, Option (1) is the suitable option.
explanationHi:
The correct option is The kid begged her mother not to leave her alone there.

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Direct speech, and we have to convert it into Indirect speech.
  • In indirect speech, the tense, pronouns, and time expressions are changed according to the rules of reported speech.
  • First, we change the reporting verb "told" into the reporting verb "begged" to convey the sense of request in the sentence.
  • Then we change the pronoun "me" to "her" because we are reporting someone else's words.
  • Next, we have to change the tense of the reported speech.
  • don't changes to 'not to'.
  • 'to' is followed by V1 form of the verb i.e. leave.
  • Finally, we change "here" to "there" because we are reporting what someone said in a different location.
  • Thus, the correct answer is Option 1.

Therefore, the correct conversion is: The kid begged her mother not to leave her alone there.

Additional Information

  • The original direct speech is in present tense and indicates a kid requesting her mother not to leave her alone.
  • Option 2 is not suitable because it keeps the tense of the statement the same without changing it to past tense and so is incorrect.
  • Option 3 is not suitable because it keeps the location 'here' as it is but in indirect tense it should change to 'there' thus making it incorrect.
  • Option 4 is not suitable because it changes the tense to present tense but in indirect speech it should be past tense, making it the incorrect option.
  • Hence, Option (1) is the suitable option.

textEn:
Given below are six sentences, out of which sentences A and F are in the correct position. Sentences B, C, D and E are in a jumbled order. Select the option that gives the correct order.

A. Discipline is not a thing to be thrust upon our boys and girls from outside.
B. On the contrary, it is purely based on their conscience.
C. It is not at all based on fear or punishment as forced discipline.
D. This type of discipline is called self-discipline.
E. It must come automatically from the core of their heart.
F. It should be controlled by the love and affection of the teachers.
textHi:
Given below are six sentences, out of which sentences A and F are in the correct position. Sentences B, C, D and E are in a jumbled order. Select the option that gives the correct order.

A. Discipline is not a thing to be thrust upon our boys and girls from outside.
B. On the contrary, it is purely based on their conscience.
C. It is not at all based on fear or punishment as forced discipline.
D. This type of discipline is called self-discipline.
E. It must come automatically from the core of their heart.
F. It should be controlled by the love and affection of the teachers.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is option 2) i.e. "AEDCBF."

Key Points
  • In the abovementioned paragraph, there is a chronological sequence of the sentences.
  • Sentence A is the first sentence as per the given instructions. It begins with the noun 'discipline' and describes it can not be imposed forcefully.
  • Sentence E is the second sentence as it further explains the above-mentioned point.
  • Sentence D is the third sentence. It defines the above-mentioned points as self-discipline.
  • Sentence C is the fourth sentence as it describes the contrary part that fear or punishment are futile tools to make someone disciplined.
  • Sentence B is the fifth sentence as it mentions the reason why discipline cannot be forced.
  • Sentence F is the last sentence as per the given instructions. It describes that to teach someone discipline a teacher needs to act with love and affection.
  • The correct arrangement is AEDCBF.

The correct order is:

  • A. Discipline is not a thing to be thrust upon our boys and girls from outside.
  • E. It must come automatically from the core of their heart.
  • D. This type of discipline is called self-discipline.
  • C. It is not at all based on fear or punishment as forced discipline.
  • B. On the contrary, it is purely based on their conscience.
  • F. It should be controlled by the love and affection of the teachers.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is option 2) i.e. "AEDCBF."

Key Points
  • In the abovementioned paragraph, there is a chronological sequence of the sentences.
  • Sentence A is the first sentence as per the given instructions. It begins with the noun 'discipline' and describes it can not be imposed forcefully.
  • Sentence E is the second sentence as it further explains the above-mentioned point.
  • Sentence D is the third sentence. It defines the above-mentioned points as self-discipline.
  • Sentence C is the fourth sentence as it describes the contrary part that fear or punishment are futile tools to make someone disciplined.
  • Sentence B is the fifth sentence as it mentions the reason why discipline cannot be forced.
  • Sentence F is the last sentence as per the given instructions. It describes that to teach someone discipline a teacher needs to act with love and affection.
  • The correct arrangement is AEDCBF.

The correct order is:

  • A. Discipline is not a thing to be thrust upon our boys and girls from outside.
  • E. It must come automatically from the core of their heart.
  • D. This type of discipline is called self-discipline.
  • C. It is not at all based on fear or punishment as forced discipline.
  • B. On the contrary, it is purely based on their conscience.
  • F. It should be controlled by the love and affection of the teachers.

textEn:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Let Arpit be appointed the monitor this time.
textHi:
Select the correct active form of the given sentence.

Let Arpit be appointed the monitor this time.
optionsEn: We request you to appoint Arpit the monitor this time.
We will appoint Arpit the monitor this time.
Appoint Arpit the monitor this time.
Arpit should be appointed the monitor this time.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Appoint Arpit the monitor this time.'
Key PointsThe given sentence is an imperative sentence.
  • To convert from the passive to the active form, we need to follow some basic rules.
  • First, we need to remove let.
  • Second, use only 'verb 1st form' instead of 'be + verb 3rd form'.
Now following the above-mentioned rules, the sentence would be - 'Appoint Arpit the monitor this time.'
Hence, the correct answer is option 3.
Additional InformationLet's understand the concept with some more examples.
  • Let it be carried home.
    • Carry it home.
  • Let the door be opened.
    • Open the door.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'Appoint Arpit the monitor this time.'
Key PointsThe given sentence is an imperative sentence.
  • To convert from the passive to the active form, we need to follow some basic rules.
  • First, we need to remove let.
  • Second, use only 'verb 1st form' instead of 'be + verb 3rd form'.
Now following the above-mentioned rules, the sentence would be - 'Appoint Arpit the monitor this time.'
Hence, the correct answer is option 3.
Additional InformationLet's understand the concept with some more examples.
  • Let it be carried home.
    • Carry it home.
  • Let the door be opened.
    • Open the door.

textEn:
Recast this active sentence into its passive form:

"The dog chased the cat."
textHi:
Recast this active sentence into its passive form:

"The dog chased the cat."
optionsEn: The cat was being chased by the dog
The dog was chased by the cat
The cat had been chased by the dog.
The cat was chased by the dog.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is: 'The cat was chased by the dog.'

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in the past simple tense in Active Voice.
  • To change a past simple tense from Active voice to Passive voice, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence and the subject becomes the 'agent' or doer of the action.
  • Structure:
    • Subject + past simple form of the verb + object (Active Voice)
    • Object + was/were + past participle + by subject (Passive Voice)
  • In the given sentence:
    • The subject of the sentence is 'The dog'.
    • The object of the sentence is 'the cat'.
  • Applying the rule changes, 'chased' will be changed into 'was chased'.

Correct sentence: The cat was chased by the dog.

Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 4'.

textEn:
Give below are six sentences four of which are jumbled. The first and the last sentences are given. Pick the option that gives the logically correct order of the four sentences.

S-1: Buddhism as a religion developed from the teachings of Siddharth Gautam.

A: The real goal of Buddhism is to attain Nirvana.

B: Initially the religion had no God as in other religions of that time.

C: But later on the followers developed Buddha, Bodhisattvas, Arhats and Deities as God.

D: Gautam Buddha or Buddha as commonly known was born in 520 BCE in North East India.

S-6: This is to free oneself from the cycle of birth, death and rebirth.
textHi:
Give below are six sentences four of which are jumbled. The first and the last sentences are given. Pick the option that gives the logically correct order of the four sentences.

S-1: Buddhism as a religion developed from the teachings of Siddharth Gautam.

A: The real goal of Buddhism is to attain Nirvana.

B: Initially the religion had no God as in other religions of that time.

C: But later on the followers developed Buddha, Bodhisattvas, Arhats and Deities as God.

D: Gautam Buddha or Buddha as commonly known was born in 520 BCE in North East India.

S-6: This is to free oneself from the cycle of birth, death and rebirth.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'DBCA'.

Key Points

  • While arranging the parts of the passage, we should find some grammatical or contextual connections between them-
    • D follows sentence 1 as sentence 1 introduces the religion Buddhism and its founder and sentence D further gives the description about Siddharth Gautam.
    • Sentences B and C form a mandatory pair as they mean that although Buddhism had no God in the beginning, later on, the followers of this religion developed Buddha, Bodhisattvas, Arhats and Deities as their God.
    • A is the last sentence as it forms a mandatory pair with sentence 6. In sentence 6, the pronoun 'this' is referred to 'Nirvana'. It means that 'Nirvana' is to free oneself from the cycle of birth, death and rebirth.


Thus, the correct order is DBCA:

  • 1: Buddhism as a religion developed from the teachings of Siddharth Gautam.
  • D: Gautam Buddha or Buddha as commonly known was born in 520 BCE in North East India.
  • B: Initially the religion had no God as in other religions of that time.
  • C: But later on the followers developed Buddha, Bodhisattvas, Arhats and Deities as God.
  • A: The real goal of Buddhism is to attain Nirvana.
  • 6: This is to free oneself from the cycle of birth, death and rebirth.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'DBCA'.

Key Points

  • While arranging the parts of the passage, we should find some grammatical or contextual connections between them-

    • Sentences B and C form a mandatory pair as they mean that although Buddhism had no God in the beginning, later on, the followers of this religion developed Buddha, Bodhisattvas, Arhats and Deities as their God.
    • A is the last sentence as it forms a mandatory pair with sentence 6. In sentence 6, the pronoun 'this' is referred to 'Nirvana'. It means that 'Nirvana' is to free oneself from the cycle of birth, death and rebirth.


Thus, the correct order is DBCA:

  • 1: Buddhism as a religion developed from the teachings of Siddharth Gautam.
  • D: Gautam Buddha or Buddha as commonly known was born in 520 BCE in North East India.
  • B: Initially the religion had no God as in other religions of that time.
  • C: But later on the followers developed Buddha, Bodhisattvas, Arhats and Deities as God.
  • A: The real goal of Buddhism is to attain Nirvana.
  • 6: This is to free oneself from the cycle of birth, death and rebirth.

textEn:
Select the most appropriate synonym of 'amazement' to fill the blank.

Leena was filled with _______ when she saw the diamond ring as her wedding gift.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate synonym of 'amazement' to fill the blank.

Leena was filled with _______ when she saw the diamond ring as her wedding gift.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is Option 1.
Key Points
  • The word "astonishment" is a synonym of "amazement," meaning great surprise or wonder. (आश्चर्य)
  • In the context of the sentence, Leena would be filled with "astonishment" upon seeing the diamond ring as her wedding gift, which fits the scenario of experiencing a strong feeling of surprise.
  • The other options: "haughtiness" (arrogance), "measurement" (the size, length, or amount of something), and "happiness" (a state of being happy) do not convey the same meaning as "amazement."

​Therefore, the correct answer is 'Option 1'.

Complete Sentence: Leena was filled with astonishment when she saw the diamond ring as her wedding gift.

Additional Information
  • Haughtiness (अहंकार): means arrogance or being disdainfully proud. It does not fit the context of being surprised by a gift.
  • Measurement (माप): refers to the size, length, or amount of something. It is unrelated to the concept of "amazement."
  • Happiness (खुशी): means a state of being happy. While it may be a related emotion, it does not specifically convey the sense of being amazed or astonished.

textEn:
Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution required'.

In the olden days, many people use to think that the earth was flat.
textHi:
Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select 'No substitution required'.

In the olden days, many people use to think that the earth was flat.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "people used to think"

Key Points

  • In the given sentence, the usage of "use to" is incorrect instead of this we need to use "used to" hence, the correct phrase is "people used to think."
  • "used to" is a modal verb that shows past habits. (done or experienced in the past, but no longer done or experienced)
  • Let's see an example-
    • I used to eat meat, but now I'm a vegetarian.

Hence, the correct sentence is- In the olden days, many people used to think that the earth was flat.

textEn:
Fill in the blank with suitable tense.

I _______ for this book since Monday.
textHi:
Fill in the blank with suitable tense.

I _______ for this book since Monday.
explanationEn:
The correct solution is 'have been searching'.

Key Points

  • In the above sentence, in order to pick the correct tense to fill in the blank, we first have to identify the tense of the rest of the sentence.
  • In the end of the sentence, we find the word 'since' which is used to denote 'the period between the time mentioned and the time under consideration'.
  • So this makes the sentence a present perfect continuous tense,which is used to denote that an action has started in the recent past and is still continuing in the present.
  • The structure of a present perfect continuous tense is Subject+ has/have + been+ present participle+ing+ rest of the sentence.
  • So the correct option will be 'have been searching' making the sentence 'I have been searching for this book since Monday'.
  • Hence, the correct answer is Option 4.

textHi:
Directions: Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.

A: It is not one long bone from the upper arm to our wrist.

B: It is different bones joined together at the elbow.

C: Bones cannot be bent.

D: So, how do we bend our elbow?

So, how do
we bend our elbow?So, how dowe bend our elbow?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'CDAB'.

Key Points

  • Sentence C comes first as it is independent of any other sentence and introduces the matter of concern, i.e., bones.
  • Sentence D follows C as it asks a question that opposes the idea given in C.
  • Sentence A follows D as it states that the elbow can be bent because it is not one single bone.
  • Sentence B follows A as it further describes that the elbow is different bones joined together at the elbow.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • ​C: Bones cannot be bent.
    • D: So, how do we bend our elbow?
    • A: It is not one long bone from the upper arm to our wrist.
    • B: It is different bones joined together at the elbow.


Important Points

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
  • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the paragraph and eliminate the irrelevant options.
  • Try to identify the opening (independent sentence) and closing sentences of the paragraph (concluding sentence).
  • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants. Identify the pronouns connecting the sentences with one other.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is 'CDAB'.

Key Points

  • Sentence C comes first as it is independent of any other sentence and introduces the matter of concern, i.e., bones.
  • Sentence D follows C as it asks a question that opposes the idea given in C.
  • Sentence A follows D as it states that the elbow can be bent because it is not one single bone.
  • Sentence B follows A as it further describes that the elbow is different bones joined together at the elbow.
  • Thus, the sequence becomes:
    • ​C: Bones cannot be bent.
    • D: So, how do we bend our elbow?
    • A: It is not one long bone from the upper arm to our wrist.
    • B: It is different bones joined together at the elbow.


Important Points

  • Important Rules to Solve the Para Jumbles:
  • Read the sentences attentively and try to comprehend the idea or theme of the paragraph and eliminate the irrelevant options.
  • Try to identify the opening (independent sentence) and closing sentences of the paragraph (concluding sentence).
  • Try to identify the mandatory pairs. Two sentences can be connected through various connectors and other determinants. Identify the pronouns connecting the sentences with one other.

textEn:
Correct the following sentence.

The teacher said that honesty was the best policy.
textHi:
Correct the following sentence.

The teacher said that honesty was the best policy.
optionsEn: The teacher told that Honesty was the best policy.
The teacher said about the honesty.
The teacher said that honesty is the best policy.
The teacher advised the honesty as the best policy.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is The teacher said that honesty is the best policy.

Key Points

  • "The teacher said that honesty was the best policy" is grammatically incorrect in this sentence.
  • The correct usage is "The teacher said that honesty is the best policy" instead of "The teacher said that honesty was the best policy."
  • The given sentence is in the Reported speech and universal truth.
  • When the reported clause is a universal truth or any historical event or habitual action, then the tense of the indirect speech doesn't change, no matter in whatever tense the reporting verb is.

The correct sentence is: The teacher said that honesty is the best policy.

The correct answer is The teacher said that honesty is the best policy.

  • When the reported clause is a universal truth or any historical event or habitual action, then the tense of the indirect speech doesn't change, no matter in whatever tense the reporting verb is.

The correct sentence is: The teacher said that honesty is the best policy.

  • "Uninterested" refers to having or showing no feeling of interest; indifferent. (उदासीन)
    • Example: She was uninterested in the conversation and kept looking at her phone.
  • Hence, we can infer that the opposite of 'Inquisitive' is 'Uninterested'.

Therefore, the correct answer is: Option 4 i.e. Uninterested.

  • Curious (जिज्ञासु): Eager to know or learn something.
    • Example: She was curious about the new technology and wanted to learn more about it.

textEn:
Direction​: In the following question, a sentence is given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives choose the one which best expresses the sentence in Indirect/Direct Speech

"Do you know Melanie?", she asked.
textHi:
Direction​: In the following question, a sentence is given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives choose the one which best expresses the sentence in Indirect/Direct Speech

"Do you know Melanie?", she asked.
optionsEn: She asked to me if I know who Melanie is.
She said to me who is Melanie
She asked me whether I knew Melanie
She asked me If I did know Melanie
explanationEn:
The correct answer is, He asked me whether I knew Melanie

Key Points

  • The given sentence is a type of yes/no question.
  • Yes/no question expect yes or no as the answer
  • While converting such sentences to indirect speech, if or whether is used
  • The third form of the verb is used in indirect speech
  • Here knew (in indirect speech) is used in place of know (in direct speech)

Additional Information

  • A yes/no question is different from a Wh- question.
  • In a yes-no question, the quoted question begins with an auxiliary verb form such as is, are, am, do, does, has, have, can, will.
  • And the expected answer is yes or no.
  • Wh-questions are questions that begin with "wh " words: who, whose, what, when, which, why, where, and how.

​The correct sentence is, He asked me whether I knew Melanie.

  • And the expected answer is yes or no.
  • Wh-questions are questions that begin with "wh " words: who, whose, what, when, which, why, where, and how.

​The correct sentence is, He asked me whether I knew Melanie.

textEn:
Directions: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested below, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice.

Did any one ever make it clear how one operates the machine?
textHi:
Directions: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested below, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice.

Did any one ever make it clear how one operates the machine?
optionsEn: Was it ever made clear how the machine is operated?
Did any one ever make it by clear how the machine is operated?
Was ever it made clear how to operate the machine?
Was how to operate the machine ever made clear?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "Was it ever made clear how the machine is operated?"

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in active voice: "Did any one ever make it clear how one operates the machine?"
  • In turning it into passive voice, the following transformation rules apply:
    • Object becomes the subject of the sentence:
      • Here, "it" made vague in the original sentence, refers to the understanding or clarity of "how one operates the machine".
      • Hence, this vague 'it' becomes the subject in the passive voice, preferred to be represented as 'was it'.
    • The action verb 'make' in active voice becomes 'made' and 'did' is replaced with 'was' in passive.
    • 'It' is positioned after 'was' to indicate that we are asking about whether some action was performed on 'it' ('it' being the clarity about machine operation).
    • Tense is maintained:
      • In the active sentence, the verb phrase "Did...make" is in the simple past tense, so the passive sentence should also be in the simple past.
      • This is done by using "was" + past participle ('made').
    • The actor of the sentence (anyone or the person who makes it clear) is dropped as it's not specific and not vital to the sentence meaning in passive form.
    • The clause 'how one operates the machine' is a clause acting as a direct object in active sentence, in the passive it changes to 'how the machine is operated', retaining the information but changing the structure to be more fitting for a passive construction.
  • So, option 1: "Was it ever made clear how the machine is operated?" is the correct transformation into passive voice.
    • It follows all the transformation rules, properly turns the action onto the object, and gives the same meaning as the original active sentence.
    • The rest of the options do not follow these transformation rules correctly.

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 1".

textEn: Find the sentence which is correct :
textHi: Find the sentence which is correct :
optionsEn: The Chairman with the directors are to be present.
Silver as well as gold have fallen in price.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'He and I were playing'.

Key Points

  • According to Syntax, when two or more subjects are connected by the conjunction 'and', a plural verb is always used in a sentence.
  • In the sentence, "He and I were playing" there are two subjects - he and I.
  • Hence they are connected with 'and'.

Hence, "He and I were playing" this sentence is errorless.

Additional Information

  • Let us look at the error in other given sentences:
  • Two and two makes four: 'Makes' should be replaced with 'make'.
  • The Chairman with the directors are to be present: 'Are' should be replaced with 'is'. If two subjects are connected with expressions like as well as, along, in addition to, with, together with, etc then the noun placed before these expressions is considered to be the main subject.
    Also, the verb should also be in agreement with the main subject i.e. the first subject.
  • Silver as well as gold have fallen in price: 'Have' should be replaced with 'has'.

The correct answer is 'He and I were playing'.

They stood ___ the bridge, watching the sunset.
textHi:
Directions: Each of the following sentences in this section has a blank space with four options. Select whichever preposition or determiner you consider the most appropriate for the blank space and indicate your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.

They stood ___ the bridge, watching the sunset.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Option 3' i.e. 'beside'.

Key Points

  • The preposition 'beside' is used to indicate that someone or something is next to or at the side of another person or thing.
  • The preposition 'through' suggests movement within or across an area, which is not applicable in this context.
  • The preposition 'in' generally indicates being inside something, which does not fit with standing near a bridge.
  • The preposition 'from' typically indicates the point of origin or separation and does not make sense in this context where location or position is implied.

Therefore, the correct answer is 'beside'.

Additional Information

  • Through: This preposition suggests movement in one side and out of the other or across something, and is not appropriate for indicating a position near an object.
  • In: This preposition is used to express a position within an area or volume, making it unsuited for describing the position next to a bridge.
  • From: This preposition indicates the point of origin, starting point, or separation, which doesn't describe a stationary position near an object.

textEn:
Direction: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.
The engineers will have completed the bridge by next summer."
textHi:
Direction: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice.
The engineers will have completed the bridge by next summer."
optionsEn: The bridge has been completed by the engineers by next summer.
The bridge will be completed by the engineers by next summer
The bridge will have been completed by the engineers by next summer.
The bridge had been completed by the engineers by next summer.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "The bridge will have been completed by the engineers by next summer."

Key Points

  • The original sentence is in the active voice, where the subject performs the action.
  • The structure is:
    • subject + future perfect verb (will have completed) + object.
  • In a passive voice sentence, this structure changes.
  • The object moves to the beginning of the sentence, becoming the new subject, and the verb changes to match.
  • The general structure is:
    • object + auxiliary verb to be (in the correct tense) + past participle of the verb + (by + subject).
  • "The bridge" is now the focus of the sentence, "will have been completed" is the future perfect passive form of the original verb phrase, correctly projecting the completion of the action in the future, and "by the engineers" retains the original actor of the action.

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 3".

textEn:
Read the comprehension and answer below:

The idea that humans can learn during slumber dates back at least to biblical times, when God gave Jacob a glimpse of his destiny in a dream of angels climbing a ladder to heaven. But the first person to make money from the concept was Alois Benjamin Saliger, a Czech-born New York-based businessman and inventor – 'tall, spare, thin-lipped', according to a contemporary account, 'with piercing eyes and a wide forehead' – who in 1932 patented the Psycho-Phone. A phonograph fitted with a repeating mechanism and a tiny acoustic horn, the device was meant to sit by a sleeper's bed and replay spoken-word recordings at the volume of a whisper. It was marketed with disks whose titles included Prosperity, Inspiration, Normal Weight, and Mating. 'I desire an ideal mate,' Saliger intoned on the latter record. 'I radiate love. I have a fascinating and attractive personality. I have a strong sex appeal.' If the machine functioned as advertised, the user would awaken filled with irresistible confidence, ready to stride off and conquer his chosen territory.

The Psycho-Phone operated on the premise that people are as suggestible while asleep as they are under hypnosis, an unproven theory picked up by Aldous Huxley in his dystopian novel, Brave New World (1932). There, recorded messages are used to train sleeping children in the values of a soulless future society. A proud official in the book calls the new method, dubbed 'hypnopaedia' by Huxley, the 'greatest moralising and socialising force of all time'.

Although hypnopaedia was never employed for mass indoctrination in the real world, it came to be widely used as a tool for teaching new skills or changing unwanted behaviour. Scientific studies seemed to show it worked. In one study, a group of sleeping men heard a recorded list of Chinese words and their English translations; the next day, they scored significantly better on a comprehension test than a control group. In another study, 20 boys with a nail-biting habit were played the phrase 'my fingernails taste terribly bitter' 300 times a night for 54 nights; by the end of the trial, 40 per cent had reportedly overcome their vice. The technique grew particularly popular in the Soviet Union, where whole villages were said to learn foreign languages while dozing.

The backlash came in the 1950s, when scientists began using electroencephalography (EEG), in which electrodes are used to measure the brain's electrical activity. With this technique, they could finally determine whether subjects were, in fact, asleep rather than drifting near sleep or just resting. When the Rand Corporation researchers William Emmons and Charles Simon repeatedly played a list of 10 words to men whose EEGs showed an absence of alpha waves (a reliable gauge of sleep), their performance on a memory test upon awakening was no better than chance. Other EEG-monitored trials drew similar results.

optionsEn: Early studies supporting hypnopaedia did not reliably distinguish between actual sleep and mere rest or drowsiness.
The Psycho-Phone was proven to significantly improve confidence in all users.
Aldous Huxley invented the concept of sleep learning.
Learning during sleep is now widely accepted by scientists.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Option 1'.
Key Points
  • The passage explains that early studies on hypnopaedia lacked reliable methods to distinguish between subjects who were truly asleep and those who were just resting or drowsy.
    • Only with the use of EEG in the 1950s could scientists confirm the subjects' true sleep state, revealing that actual sleep learning was ineffective.
  • Option 2: The Psycho-Phone was proven to significantly improve confidence in all users.
    • Incorrect, there is no scientific evidence supporting this claim in the passage.
  • Option 3: Aldous Huxley invented the concept of sleep learning.
    • Incorrect, the passage notes the idea predates Huxley, though he popularized it in fiction.
  • Option 4: Learning during sleep is now widely accepted by scientists.
    • Incorrect, the passage states that scientific evidence did not support sleep learning.
Therefore, it can be concluded that early studies supporting hypnopaedia did not reliably distinguish between actual sleep and mere rest or drowsiness.

The technique grew particularly popular in the Soviet Union, where whole villages were said to learn foreign languages while dozing.

The backlash came in the 1950s, when scientists began using electroencephalography (EEG), in which electrodes are used to measure the brain's electrical activity. With this technique, they could finally determine whether subjects were, in fact, asleep rather than drifting near sleep or just resting. When the Rand Corporation researchers William Emmons and Charles Simon repeatedly played a list of 10 words to men whose EEGs showed an absence of alpha waves (a reliable gauge of sleep), their performance on a memory test upon awakening was no better than chance. Other EEG-monitored trials drew similar results.

What is the main idea of the passage?
optionsEn: The Psycho-Phone was the most successful invention of the 20th century.
Hypnopaedia has always been rejected by scientists.
The concept of learning during sleep, or hypnopaedia, has a long history and was once popularized and commercialized, but ultimately scientific evidence failed to support its effectiveness.
All sleep-related inventions have been unsuccessful.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Option 3'.
Key Points
  • The passage traces the history of the idea that humans can learn while asleep, focusing on hypnopaedia and inventions like the Psycho-Phone.
    • It describes the initial enthusiasm, commercialization, and seeming scientific support for sleep learning, followed by the eventual scientific debunking using EEG technology.
  • Option 1: The Psycho-Phone was the most successful invention of the 20th century.
    • Incorrect, the passage does not claim this.
  • Option 2: Hypnopaedia has always been rejected by scientists.
    • Incorrect, it was once widely accepted and promoted before being disproved.
  • Option 4: All sleep-related inventions have been unsuccessful.
    • Incorrect, the passage only critiques sleep-learning, not all sleep inventions.
Therefore, the main idea of the passage is the concept of learning during sleep, or hypnopaedia, has a long history and was once popularized and commercialized, but ultimately scientific evidence failed to support its effectiveness.

textEn:
In the following question, a question is given in Direct speech, Out of the four alternatives, choose the which best expresses the sentence in Indirect speech.
Raja said to, "Suraj, I am waiting for you".
textHi:
In the following question, a question is given in Direct speech, Out of the four alternatives, choose the which best expresses the sentence in Indirect speech.
Raja said to, "Suraj, I am waiting for you".
optionsEn: Raja told Suraj that he was waiting for him.
Raja said that he was waiting for him
Raja told Suraj that he is waiting for him.
Raja told Suraj that he had been waiting for him.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is option 1)i.e. Raja told Suraj that he was waiting for him.

Explanation:

  • This is an assertive sentence.
  • It will be changed into Indirect speech by making the following changes:
    • Comma and inverted commas are removed.
    • The conjunction that is used.
    • First person pronouns in the direct speech change according to the subject(Raja) of the reporting verb(said'. Hence, 'I' changes to 'he'.
    • Present tense is changed to past. Hence, 'am waiting' changes to 'was waiting'.

Important Points

  • Sometimes the object of reporting verb is not mentioned but it can be guessed.
  • Here, we know that the object of the reporting verb is Suraj.

Eventually, we get the final sentence- "Raja told Suraj that he was waiting for him.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is option 1)i.e. Raja told Suraj that he was waiting for him.

Explanation:

  • This is an assertive sentence.

    • Comma and inverted commas are removed.

    • First person pronouns in the direct speech change according to the subject(Raja) of the reporting verb(said'. Hence, 'I' changes to 'he'.
    • Present tense is changed to past. Hence, 'am waiting' changes to 'was waiting'.

Important Points

  • Sometimes the object of reporting verb is not mentioned but it can be guessed.
  • Here, we know that the object of the reporting verb is Suraj.

Eventually, we get the final sentence- "Raja told Suraj that he was waiting for him.

textEn:
In the following question, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Indirect/Direct speech.

The landlady said to the tenant," Every room is fitted with an electric fan".
textHi:
In the following question, a sentence has been given in Direct/Indirect speech. Out of the four alternatives suggested, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Indirect/Direct speech.

The landlady said to the tenant," Every room is fitted with an electric fan".
optionsEn: The landlady said to the tenant that every room is fitted with an electric fan.
The landlady told the tenant that every room was fitted with an electric fan.
The landlady told the tenant that every room is fitted with an electric fan.
The landlady told the tenant that every room has been fitted with an electric fan.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is option 2) i.e. The landlady told the tenant that every room was fitted with an electric fan.

Explanation:

  • This is an assertive sentence.
  • We change an assertive sentence in direct into indirect by making the following changes:
    • The reporting verb 'said to' becomes told.
    • Comma and inverted commas are removed.
    • The conjunction 'that' is used after the object(tenant) of the reporting verb.
    • Is becomes was.

Important Points

  • Present changes into the Past.
  • Past Tense of 'is' is 'was'.

Eventually, we will get the final sentence- "The landlady told the tenant that every room was fitted with an electric fan."
explanationHi:
The correct answer is option 2) i.e. The landlady told the tenant that every room was fitted with an electric fan.

    • The conjunction 'that' is used after the object(tenant) of the reporting verb.
    • Is becomes was.

Important Points

  • Present changes into the Past.
  • Past Tense of 'is' is 'was'.

Eventually, we will get the final sentence- "The landlady told the tenant that every room was fitted with an electric fan."

textEn:
"We will arrive late," they said.

Which is the correct indirect speech for the sentence above?
textHi:
"We will arrive late," they said.

Which is the correct indirect speech for the sentence above?
optionsEn: They said they would arrive late.
They said that they arrive late.
They said we will arrive late.
They said you will arrive late.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'They said they would arrive late.'

Key Points

  • When changing from direct to indirect speech, we typically shift from future tense to conditional tense.
  • So, 'will' changes to 'would'.
  • The first person plural 'we' changes to the third person plural 'they' referring to the same group of people.
  • The order of words 'They said' remains the same in indirect speech.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option a.

Additional Information

  • This change from 'will' to 'would' is part of the general shifting of tenses when we turn direct speech into indirect speech.

textEn:
Directions: In this section, each sentence is incomplete and is followed by four options. Select one of the four options to complete the sentence.

You remember the first place we ate _______.

Directions: In this section, each sentence is incomplete and is followed by four options. Select one of the four options to complete the sentence.

You remember the first place we ate _______.
optionsEn: didn't we?
was it?
can it be?
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "don't you?"

Key Points

  • The sentence structure you've presented indicates a question tagged onto the end of a statement. This is a common form in English known as a 'tag question.' The purpose of tag questions is to either confirm the information in the statement or to genuinely ask a question.
  • When the main part of the sentence is positive, the tag at the end is usually negative, and vice versa. In your sentence, "You remember the first place we ate" is a positive statement, so a negative tag ("don't you?") is expected to follow.
  • The choice "don't you?" fits grammatically because the main verb in the preceding statement is "remember," which requires the auxiliary verb "do" in simple present tense questions or negations. Since the statement is in affirmatory form, the tag becomes "don't you?" to make it negative, following the common structure for forming tag questions.

Hence, the correct answer is 'Option 3"

textEn:
Direction: Select the most appropriate homonym to fill in the blank.

Prices reach a ______ during August.
textHi:
Direction: Select the most appropriate homonym to fill in the blank.

Prices reach a ______ during August.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is- 'Peak.'

Key Points

  • Words like "peek", "peak", "pick", and "pique" are called homophones.
  • This means that they sound the same but have different spellings and meanings.
  • Let us see the meanings of the words:
    • Peek: to look at something quickly and secretly because you should not be looking at it. (किसी व्‍यक्ति या घटना को एक क्षण के लिए तथा छिप कर देखना (विशेषतः जब इस प्रकार देखना अनुचित है))
    • Peak: the point at which something is the highest, best, strongest, etc. (वह बिंदु जहाँ कोई वस्‍तु उच्‍चतम, सर्वोत्तम, प्रबलतम आदि स्थिति में हो)
    • Pick: to choose somebody/something from a group of people or things. ((व्‍यक्तियों या वस्‍तुओं के समूह में से) चुनना, चयन करना, छाँटना)
    • Pique: arouse (interest or curiosity). (जगाना (रुचि या जिज्ञासा))
  • The given sentence talks about August marking the highest month for prices.
  • The option "Peak" is correct in this context.

Correct sentence: Prices reach a peak during August.

textEn: Choose the grammatically correct sentence.

optionsEn: My friends house is big green, and expensive.

explanationEn:

Key Points

  • The statement in option 1 is an example of interrogative sentence.
    • The structure for it is: wh- word+ subject+ auxiliary verb+ other info.
    • In the given statement the error lies in the usage of auxiliary verb 'is'.
    • The auxiliary verb isn't following subject, here in the sentence the subject is 'time' which makes this statement incorrect.
  • The statement in option 2 is an example of apostrophe usage.
    • The meaning of apostrophe is a mark ' used to indicate the omission of letters or figures, the possessive case (as in 'John's book'), or the plural of letters or figures (as in 'the 1960's').
    • Here, in the sentence the apostrophe mark is not used to show the possession which makes the statement incorrect.
  • The statement in option 3 is an example of simple present tense.
    • The structure for it: Subject+ do/does+ not+ the first form of verb+ object.
    • From the structure, we can say that the usage of 'needs' is incorrect in the sentence.
  • The statement in option 4 is grammatically and contextually correct.
  • The subject of the sentence, i.e. 'Ram' is singular and is succeeded by singular verb.
  • Thus, the correct answer is option 4.

Key Points

  • "Quiet" refers to making very little or no noise; it is the opposite of talking excessively or noisily. (शांत)
    • Example: The library is a quiet place where people can read and study without distractions.
  • Hence, we can infer that the opposite of 'babble' is 'quiet'.

Therefore, the correct answer is: Option 2 i.e. Quiet.

    • Example: She loves to gossip about her coworkers during lunch breaks.
  • Talkative (बातूनी): Fond of or given to talking a lot.
    • Example: He is a talkative person who enjoys long conversations.
  • Rants (प्रलाप): Speaking or shouting at length in an angry, impassioned way.
    • Example: He went on a rant about the poor service he received at the restaurant.

textEn:
Directions: In this section, each sentence is incomplete and is followed by four options. Select one of the four options to complete the sentence.

Given the rapid technological advancements, businesses must constantly innovate _______.
textHi:
Directions: In this section, each sentence is incomplete and is followed by four options. Select one of the four options to complete the sentence.

Given the rapid technological advancements, businesses must constantly innovate _______.
optionsEn: otherwise, they face obsolescence
unless they face obsolescence.
while they face obsolescence.
if they face obsolescence
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "otherwise, they face obsolescence."

Key Points

  • The correct answer "otherwise, they face obsolescence" clearly conveys a consequence of not innovating amidst rapid technological advancements. It indicates that failure to innovate will likely lead to becoming outdated or obsolete.
  • "Otherwise" is used to introduce an alternative outcome, essentially warning against the consequences of not adhering to the advice given in the first part of the sentence. It fits perfectly to express the notion that businesses need to avoid the negative outcome (obsolescence) by constantly innovating.
  • The other options do not convey the sense of urgency and consequence as effectively as "otherwise." "Unless" suggests a condition, but it doesn't fit the cause-effect relationship needed here. "While" suggests simultaneity, not consequence, and "if" introduces a condition that doesn't follow the logical structure of a warning or consequence.
  • Using "otherwise" emphasizes the direct correlation between the need for constant innovation and the risk of becoming obsolete, offering a clear, impactful statement on the importance of adaptation and innovation in the business world.
Hence, the correct answer is 'Option 1'

textEn:
Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence.

It is one of the few introduced species that can successfully invades and colonize a virgin forest.
textHi:
Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence.

It is one of the few introduced species that can successfully invades and colonize a virgin forest.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'that can successfully invade'.

Key Points

  • The error lies in the incorrect usage of the verb 'invades'.
  • The verb of a sentence depends on the subject of the sentence. If the subject is singular then the verb will be singular and if the subject is plural then the verb will be plural according to the subject-verb agreement.
    • Example:
      • They are playing cricket.
      • He is playing cricket.
  • The pronoun 'that' is an antecedent in the given sentence and therefore it refers to the subject of the sentence which is 'species'.
  • The subject of the sentence is plural therefore, the verb will also be plural i.e. invade.
  • Therefore, the correct answer is option 1.

Correct sentence: It is one of the few introduced species that can successfully invade and colonize a virgin forest.

Additional Information

  • Antecedent: An antecedent is a noun or pronoun to which another noun or pronoun refers.

textEn:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.

The teacher asked me why I had reached the examination hall so late.
textHi:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.

The teacher asked me why I had reached the examination hall so late.
optionsEn: The teacher said to me, "Why are you reaching the examination hall so late?"
The teacher said to me, "Why had I reached the examination hall so late?"
The teacher said to me, "Why did you reach the examination hall so late?"
The teacher said to me, "Why you reached the examination hall so late?"
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'The teacher said to me, "Why did you reach the examination hall so late?"

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in Indirect Speech. As per the question we have to change it into Direct Speech.
  • The process of transformation is as follows:
    • 'Asked' will be changed into 'said to' because the given question is an example of an interrogative sentence with a question word.
    • Commas and inverted commas will be added.
    • 'had reached' will be changed into 'did reach'. Because in 'Direct to Indirect' transformation 'simple past' tense is changed into 'past perfect tense'.
    • 'I' will be changed into 'you' because in 'direct to indirect' transformation first-person pronoun is changed according to the subject of the reporting verb.
    • 'Why' will be used as a conjunction because we know that in an interrogative sentence with a question word, the question word itself is used as a conjunction.
    • Therefore, as per the points mentioned above, we find that the correct answer is Option 3.

Correct Sentence: The teacher said to me, "Why did you reach the examination hall so late?"

textEn: He does not abide __________ the rule.
textHi: He does not abide __________ the rule.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "by".

Key Points

  • In English, the correct preposition to use with "abide" in this context is "by."
    • ​So the correct sentence would be: "He does not abide by the rule."
  • The verb "abide" essentially means to accept or act in accordance with a rule, decision, or recommendation. And when used in this sense, it typically is accompanied by the preposition "by."

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 4".

Additional Information

  • Here's why the other options are incorrect:
    • at: The preposition "at" is not grammatically correct in this context. Although "at" can indicate a location or a point in time, it isn't used with "abide" to indicate following a rule.
    • of: The preposition "of" can denote a relationship between a part and a whole, an origin, or a cause, among other things. However, it is not used with "abide" in the sense of adhering to a rule.
    • for: Although "for" is used to indicate the purpose of an action, it is not used with the verb "abide" to convey the meaning of obeying or following a rule.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is "by".

Key Points

  • In English, the correct preposition to use with "abide" in this context is "by."
    • ​So the correct sentence would be: "He does not abide by the rule."
  • The verb "abide" essentially means to accept or act in accordance with a rule, decision, or recommendation. And when used in this sense, it typically is accompanied by the preposition "by."

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 4".

Additional Information

  • Here's why the other options are incorrect:
    • at: The preposition "at" is not grammatically correct in this context. Although "at" can indicate a location or a point in time, it isn't used with "abide" to indicate following a rule.
    • of: The preposition "of" can denote a relationship between a part and a whole, an origin, or a cause, among other things. However, it is not used with "abide" in the sense of adhering to a rule.
    • for: Although "for" is used to indicate the purpose of an action, it is not used with the verb "abide" to convey the meaning of obeying or following a rule.

  • Structure:

  • Rule 2:
    • The first part of the sentence is in the Present Continuous Tense, so the second part must be in the same tense to maintain the uniformity of the sentence.
  • So, the correct answer is Option 4.

textEn:
Transform the following sentence into the passive voice:

They had completed the project before the deadline.
textHi:
Transform the following sentence into the passive voice:

They had completed the project before the deadline.
optionsEn: The project had been completed by them before the deadline.
The project was completed by them before the deadline.
The project were completed by them before the deadline.
The project completed by them before the deadline.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is: 'The project had been completed by them before the deadline.'


Key Points

  • The given sentence is in the Past Perfect Tense and in Active Voice.
  • To change a Past Perfect Tense from Active voice to Passive voice: Interchange the object and the subject with each other - object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence.
  • Structure:
    • Subject + had + V3 + object (Active Voice)
    • Object + had been + V3 + by Subject (Passive Voice)
  • Example:
    • They had completed the project before the deadline. (Active Voice)
    • The project had been completed by them before the deadline. (Passive Voice)
  • The subject of the given sentence is 'They.'
  • The object of the given sentence is 'the project.'
  • Apply the rule changes - 'had completed' will be changed into 'had been completed'.

The correct answer is option 1.

Correct sentence: The project had been completed by them before the deadline.

The correct answer is: 'The project had been completed by them before the deadline.'

  • Structure:
    • Subject + had + V3 + object (Active Voice)
    • Object + had been + V3 + by Subject (Passive Voice)

    • They had completed the project before the deadline. (Active Voice)

  • The object of the given sentence is 'the project.'
  • Apply the rule changes - 'had completed' will be changed into 'had been completed'.

The correct answer is option 1.

Correct sentence: The project had been completed by them before the deadline.

textEn:
Directions: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested below, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice.

People are raising a hue and cry and are breaking the furniture.
textHi:
Directions: A sentence has been given in Active/Passive Voice. Out of the four alternatives suggested below, select the one which best expresses the same sentence in Passive/Active Voice.

People are raising a hue and cry and are breaking the furniture.
optionsEn: A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people.
A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture are being broken by the people.
Hue and cry and the furniture is being broken.
A hue and cry has been raised and the furniture has been broke.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people."

Key Points

  • The given sentence is in the active voice, and we have to convert it into a passive voice.
  • Before evaluating the options, let's understand the structure of active and passive voices.
    • Active voice structure: Subject (doer of action) + Verb + Object (receiver of action)
    • Passive voice structure: Object (as subject) + Auxiliary verb ('be' variant as per tense) + Main Verb (past participle) + "by" + Agent ()
  • Active voice sentence: People are raising a hue and cry and are breaking the furniture.
  • In this sentence:
    • Subject: People Verb: are raising, are breaking Object: a hue and cry, the furniture
  • We should preserve the tense ('are raising' i.e., present continuous tense) while converting the sentence.
  • Passive voice form of the above sentence would be:
    • "A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people."
  • Now, let's assess each option:
    • 1)"A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people." This is the correct conversion into the passive voice. It retains the original meaning and tense.
    • 2)"A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture are being broken by the people." This sentence incorrectly uses 'are' for 'furniture', it should be 'is' because 'furniture' is an uncountable noun.
    • 3)"Hue and cry and the furniture is being broken." This sentence is incomplete and does not correctly represent the passive form.
    • 4)"A hue and cry has been raised and the furniture has been broke." This sentence uses a tense (present perfect) that doesn't match the original sentence (present continuous), and 'broke' is incorrect; it should be 'broken'.
  • Based on the analysis, the correct alternative is option 1) "A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people." which correctly expresses the sentence in the passive voice.

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 1".
explanationHi:
The correct answer is "A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people."

  • The given sentence is in the active voice, and we have to convert it into a passive voice.

    • Passive voice structure: Object (as subject) + Auxiliary verb ('be' variant as per tense) + Main Verb (past participle) + "by" + Agent ()
  • Active voice sentence: People are raising a hue and cry and are breaking the furniture.
  • In this sentence:

  • We should preserve the tense ('are raising' i.e., present continuous tense) while converting the sentence.
  • Passive voice form of the above sentence would be:
    • "A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people."
  • Now, let's assess each option:
    • 1)"A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture is being broken by the people." This is the correct conversion into the passive voice. It retains the original meaning and tense.
    • 2)"A hue and cry is being raised and the furniture are being broken by the people." This sentence incorrectly uses 'are' for 'furniture', it should be 'is' because 'furniture' is an uncountable noun.
    • 3)"Hue and cry and the furniture is being broken." This sentence is incomplete and does not correctly represent the passive form.
    • 4)"A hue and cry has been raised and the furniture has been broke." This sentence uses a tense (present perfect) that doesn't match the original sentence (present continuous), and 'broke' is incorrect; it should be 'broken'.

Therefore, the correct answer is "Option 1".

  • Systematic: means done according to a plan or system; does not imply merciless actions.

textEn:

It is rare because it is the only such a map available.
textHi:

It is rare because it is the only such a map available.
explanationEn:
Here, the erroneous part is 'such a map.'

Key Points

  • The error lies in part (such a map) of the sentence.
  • The meaning of the phrase "such a" is one of this or that kind : one of a specified or understood kind.
    • Example: She is such a liar.
  • ​The word "only" is used to show that there is a single one or very few of something, or that there are no others.
    • Example: I was the only person on the train.​
  • So, we need to remove "such a" from the sentence to make it grammatically correct.
  • Therefore, the correct answer is Option 4.

Correct sentence: It is rare because it is the only map available.

textEn: They have come a long way. In past perfect tense this will read as:

  • The past perfect, also called the pluperfect, is a verb tense used to talk about actions that were completed before some point in the past.
  • The formula for the past perfect tense is had + [past participle]. It doesn't matter if the subject is singular or plural; the formula doesn't change.
    • Example: They had used all the washable paint.
  • Now, among the given options:
    • The sentence in option 3 is constructed in the pattern: subject+ had+ past participle of the verb "come"+ rest of the sentence.
  • Thus, it can be concluded that the given sentence in past perfect tense will be read as:
    • They had came a long way.

Hence, the correct answer is option 3.

textEn:
Choose the option that is the indirect form of the sentence.

The hostel warden said to the inspector, "Rahul arrived on Sunday from the holiday."
textHi:
Choose the option that is the indirect form of the sentence.

The hostel warden said to the inspector, "Rahul arrived on Sunday from the holiday."
optionsEn: The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul will arrive on Sunday from the holiday.
The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul has arrived on Sunday from the holiday.
The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul had arrived on Sunday from the holiday.
The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul is going to arrive on Sunday from the holiday.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is "The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul had arrived on Sunday from the holiday."

Key Points

  • When the reporting speech is in Simple Past Tense then all the speech Changes to Past Perfect tense.
    • Arrived ----- Had arrived
  • 'Said to' of the reporting verb changed into 'told.'
  • Replace comma (,) and inverted commas (" ") with the conjunction 'that'.
  • The third person(Rohit) of the reporting speech remains unchanged.

  • ​Thus, the final sentence is: The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul had arrived on Sunday from the holiday.

Additional Information

  • What is Direct & Indirect Speech?
  • Direct speech – reporting the message of the speaker in the exact words as spoken by him.
    • Direct speech example: Anjali said, "I am writing now".
  • Indirect speech: reporting the message of the speaker in our own words
    • Indirect speech example: Anjali said that she was writing then.
explanationHi:
The correct answer is "The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul had arrived on Sunday from the holiday."

Key Points

  • When the reporting speech is in Simple Past Tense then all the speech Changes to Past Perfect tense.
    • Arrived ----- Had arrived
  • 'Said to' of the reporting verb changed into 'told.'
  • Replace comma (,) and inverted commas (" ") with the conjunction 'that'.
  • The third person(Rohit) of the reporting speech remains unchanged.

  • ​Thus, the final sentence is: The hostel warden told the inspector that Rahul had arrived on Sunday from the holiday.

Additional Information

  • What is Direct & Indirect Speech?
  • Direct speech – reporting the message of the speaker in the exact words as spoken by him.
    • Direct speech example: Anjali said, "I am writing now".
  • Indirect speech: reporting the message of the speaker in our own words
    • Indirect speech example: Anjali said that she was writing then.

In Indian mythology, most of the Asuras wish to be never able to die.
textHi:
Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words.

In Indian mythology, most of the Asuras wish to be never able to die.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'Option 4'.

Key Points

  • The word "immortal" means "never able to die," which accurately fits the context of the sentence.
  • Options like "invincible," "impervious," and "invisible" do not relate directly to the idea of not dying, so they are incorrect choices.

Hence, the correct answer is 'Option 4'.

Correct Sentence: In Indian mythology, most of the Asuras wish to be immortal.

Additional Information

  • Option 1: "Invincible" means "unable to be defeated" rather than "unable to die," which is not correct in this context.
  • Option 2: "Impervious" means "not affected by something" and does not fit the context of death or immortality.
  • Option 3: "Invisible" means "unable to be seen" and is unrelated to the concept of not dying.

textEn:
A sentence has been given with a blank to be filled with an appropriate word. Choose the correct alternative.

Many people ________ the sweeper for not keeping the roads clear.
textHi:
A sentence has been given with a blank to be filled with an appropriate word. Choose the correct alternative.

Many people ________ the sweeper for not keeping the roads clear.
explanationEn:
The correct answer is 'blamed'.

Key Points

  • Let us look into the meaning of the given options -
    • praised - express warm approval or admiration of.
    • claimed - state or assert that something is the case, typically without providing evidence or proof.
    • calmed - make (someone) tranquil and quiet; soothe.
    • blamed - feel or declare that (someone or something) is responsible for a fault or wrong.
  • The given sentence implies that in the people's opinion, the sweeper was not clearing the roads.
  • Thus we can infer that the sweeper was at fault as per the people.
  • Hence, 'blamed' is the appropriate word for the given blank.
  • So, option 4 is the correct answer.

Correct sentence - Many people blamed the sweeper for not keeping the roads clear.
